body.title: agent_datacenter: lock queue overflow default and create DeviceConfig dataclass
body.result: DeviceConfig dataclass in config/device_config.py. drop-oldest default, drop_newest override, restart loop protection fields. to_dict() serializes cleanly. Tests pass.
body.description:
Lock the rack-level queue overflow policy and create the DeviceConfig dataclass that all device implementations import.

Decision confirmed by Akien 2026-04-27:
- Rack default: drop-oldest (agent traffic is typically state/status; newer supersedes older)
- Per-device override: DeviceConfig(drop_newest=True) for order-preserving pipelines

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/config/device_config.py:
  @dataclass
  class DeviceConfig:
    max_queue_length: int = 100
    drop_newest: bool = False  # rack default = drop-oldest
    max_restart_failures: int = 3
    restart_window_seconds: int = 60
    restart_backoff_seconds: float = 5.0
    manual_block_only: bool = False  # set True for Igor (no auto-unblock)

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/config/device_config.py (new)

Design rules:
- oop-first: DeviceConfig is a dataclass with typed fields and defaults, not a dict or ini file
- docs-in-code: module docstring explains each field and the drop-oldest rationale

Scope boundary:
IN: DeviceConfig dataclass with all per-device policy fields
OUT: queue implementation code (T-adc-bounded-inbound-queue, Phase 1)

Test plan:
- DeviceConfig() instantiates with all defaults
- DeviceConfig(drop_newest=True) overrides correctly
- dataclasses.asdict(DeviceConfig()) serializes cleanly (for flat-file registry)
